title: The Oracle Story Generator - a tool for creating adventures and campaigns (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nwbp-b7PO4g

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines The Oracle Story Generator from Nord Games, a box set of five card decks themed around contracts and bounties, simple side quests, relics and artifacts, epic adventures, and political intrigue. Each deck splits into five sub-decks, and the generative engine is simple: draw one card each for actor, action, subject, intent and development to seed a story, then roll the D4 sub-chart on any card for extra nuance.

He demonstrates by spinning up three plots live. A scholar racing to destroy a poisonous swamp before a dormant creature regenerates becomes a layered side quest about draining wetlands and surviving noxious gas. A boastful merchant's "trick item" of revenge turns into a monkey's-paw demon of greed waiting to devour his empire. And an epic seed — a cursed warlord bribing the Specter of Death for immortality, guarded by a legion of constructs — sketches a whole campaign's big bad. The decks even mix and match thanks to differing card backs.

His one substantial criticism: the instruction manual wastes too many pages on pre-written sample stories when it could have packed in more random generators — the actual reason to buy a tool like this. Still, at around $40 he considers it good value.

----

Do you reach for structured "draw one of each" story prompts like these, or do you prefer freeform oracles that leave more of the connective tissue to you?
